title: Baccaurea lanceolata

intro: Baccaurea lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaved baccaurea, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its ability to help manage various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
